restore.notes: Replace module name, version, and connection metadata with values discovered from the active workspace. Reconcile any app-specific expect or restore structures against a real current blueprint before activation.

metadata.notes: Template only. Replace placeholders with discovered app and connection data before deployment. The generic API shell blueprint starts with scenario-service:StartSubscenario as the start/input module, then the app-specific API-call module, then scenario-service:ReturnData. After create or update, explicitly patch and verify the scenario-level interface before the first run, then execute the shell through /api/v2/scenarios/{scenarioId}/run with matching data keys for path, method, header, and body. For the generic shell contract, keep ReturnData mapped to {{3.body}} so ExpectDataAny receives the raw response body; validate runs to confirm body contains the intended payload or error object. If a provider module mishandles empty bodies on GET or DELETE, keep this shell contract but consider a read shell without the Module 2 body mapper and a separate write shell with it.
